title: corynaea crassa var. sprucei
intro: Corynaea crassa var. sprucei, also known as the Mexican prickly poppy, is a plant species that belongs to the Papaveraceae family and is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Corynaea crassa var. sprucei include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a variety of health conditions, including skin infections and wounds.
